close_reason: Design decision: addr= is admin-trust by design
description:
lib.rs:14-21 documents the URI trust assumption: from_uri trusts addr= completely, and constructing URIs from untrusted input is forbidden because addr= = SSRF + token theft.

A reviewer might propose 'sanitize/allowlist addr=' or 'fail closed if addr is not in some known list'. This would be wrong: secretx is a configuration layer, not a policy layer. URIs come from admin-controlled config; the trust boundary is at config load time, not at URI parse time. Adding allowlist logic here would be feature creep and would create a false sense of security (since a hostile config can still set VAULT_ADDR or the file content directly).

Defend against: 'addr= is a security risk, validate it'. The risk is documented at the right layer; mitigations belong above this crate, not inside it.
title: DECISION: addr= URI parameter is admin-trust by design and documented
